housing_pipeline.description: The proposed project involves the remodel of an existing restaurant and offices to incorporate a mixed-use conversion. The existing Formula Retail restaurant, Burger King, will remain while the existing office space will be converted into nine residential units. In addition, a 1.5-story, vertical addition with a total of four stories will be constructed to accommodate the new residential units. The proposed project employs SB-423 for streamlined approval and the California State Density Bonus law to increase the allowable unit density by 80% of base density (five units), totaling nine units. The project additionally requests waivers and incentives to achieve the additional units.

Priority Project per ED 13-01
Demolition of an existing mortuary and construction of a new 98 unit 100% affordable senior housing building with a ground floor neighborhood commercial space. No parking provided

items.title: Adopt a Motion of Support to Allocate $350,000 in Prop L Funds, with Conditions, for Two Requests
Projects: Prop L: SFMTA: District 2 Turk Boulevard Pedestrian Safety [NTP] ($200,000), Curbside Electric Vehicle Charging ($150,000)

Adopt a Motion of Support to Amend the Prop L Standard Grant Agreement for the Woods/Islais Creek Yard Electrification Phase I Project to Update the Project Scope, Schedule, Cost, and Funding Plan; and Change the Project Phase from Design to Construction
Call to Order
Chair’s Report
Item 4
Adopt a Motion of Support to Approve Programming Priorities for Up to $4,548,974 in San Francisco’s State Transit Assistance County Block Grant Funds, with Conditions
Projects: STA: BART: Elevator Attendant Program ($1,243,286). SFMTA: Paratransit Program ($2,812,954). WETA: Treasure Island Electric Ferry Service ($492,734).
Item 7
Adopt a Motion of Support to Approve the State and Federal Legislative Update
Adopt a Motion of Support to Amend the Adopted Fiscal Year 2025/26 Budget to Decrease Other Financing Sources by $50,000,000 for a Total Net Increase in Fund Balance of $50,000,000
Preliminary Fiscal Year 2025/26 Budget and Work Program — INFORMATION*
San Francisco Transportation Plan 2050+ and West Side Network Study Update - INFORMATION*
Brotherhood Way Safety and Circulation Plan Update — INFORMATION*
